Fix incorrect default package imports for payload classes by using fully qualified package names.
Add missing import for BadCredentialsException required for the new exception handling logic.
Wrap authentication logic in try-catch to handle BadCredentialsException and return a 400 response instead of a 500 error.
Change return type from specific ResponseEntity<JwtResponse> to generic ResponseEntity to allow returning error messages.

Fix incorrect import path for MessageResponse by using the fully qualified package name instead of a relative one.

Remove inheritance from the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter class to comply with Spring Security 6 requirements.
Replace the void configure(HttpSecurity) method with a @Bean method returning SecurityFilterChain, as required by Spring Security 6.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.human_impl: @Bean protected SecurityFilterChain configure(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception { ... return http.build(); }

src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Replace the deprecated authorizeRequests() DSL with the new lambda-based authorizeHttpRequests() API.
Replace deprecated antMatchers() calls with requestMatchers() to align with the new SecurityFilterChain API.
Replace the overridden authenticationManagerBean() with a new bean method using AuthenticationConfiguration, as the old approach is removed in Spring Security 6.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.human_impl: @Bean public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration config) throws Exception { return config.getAuthenticationManager(); }

src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Remove the configure(WebSecurity) method for ignoring static resources, likely moving these rules to the SecurityFilterChain or relying on default static resource handling.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.human_impl: Removed: public void configure(WebSecurity web) throws Exception { web.ignoring() ... }
